senses.definition: plural of calypter, a small membranous flap or plate on the wing of certain flies that covers the halteres.
examples: The calypterae are visible at the base of the wings in these flies.
Taxonomists note differences in calypterae when identifying species.

senses.definition: plural of calypter, a structure on the wing of a fly covering its halteres.
examples: Calypteras vary in size between species.
Some sections of flies lack calypteras altogether.

senses.definition: the calypters are the alulae or small lobes at the hind part of the wing of certain two-winged flies, which can cover the halteres.
examples: The size of the calypters helps distinguish species.
Calypters may be reduced or absent in some groups.

senses.pos: noun (plural or collective noun form, specialized)
senses.definition: an infrequent term used in zoological literature to refer to calyptoblastic entities, often in hydroid or related hydrozoan contexts.
examples: Researchers noted calyptoblastea in the specimen collection.
The term appears in taxonomic descriptions of hydrozoan groups.

senses.definition: relating to or having calyptoblastic characteristics in hydroids; pertaining to calyptoblastea.
examples: The calyptoblastic form was observed in the hydrozoan colony.
Calyptoblastic features are used in classification of certain hydroids.

senses.definition: a taxonomic genus or species name in biological classification, often used for a particular organism.
examples: Calypotohynchus is listed in the genus records.
The specimen was identified as Calyptorhynchus species.

senses.definition: a hood-like covering over a part of a plant or fungus or, in biology, a cap-like structure; in botany, the cap or covering of a moss capsule; in entomology, a flap on a fly's wing covers the halteres.
examples: The moss capsule opened when the calyptra lifted.
Calyptra in mosss help protect the spores during development.
The fly's calyptra shields the halteres.

senses.definition: a genus name used in scientific classification; used as a proper noun in taxonomy.
examples: Calyptraea is a genus of marine snails.
The species Calyptraea chinensis has a distinctive shell.

senses.definition: a genus of tropical plants; used as a scientific name in botany.
examples: Calyptranthes is native to tropical regions.
Horticulturists study Calyptranthes for ornamental value.

senses.definition: alternate plural form of calyptra or calypter in some contexts; used primarily in scientific literature.
examples: Calyptras were observed covering the halteres.
The terminology varies between authors, with calyptras and calypters used interchangeably in some texts.

senses.definition: an outdated or specialized taxonomic grouping name used in descriptions of certain fly families; related to the calypters and wing structures.
examples: Calyptrata is mentioned in older entomology texts.
Modern classifications may replace Calyptrata with more updated families.

senses.definition: the division or section of flies characterized by having calypters; a taxonomic grouping used in some scientific classifications.
examples: Calyptratae includes families with well-developed calypters.
The placement of Calyptratae has changed with new phylogenetic data.

senses.definition: having calypters; of or relating to calypters.
examples: Calyptrate flies have distinctive wing covers.
The calyptrate group is used in insect classification.

senses.definition: shaped like or having the form of a calyptra (a hood-like covering).
examples: The calyptriform structure resembles a small hood over the organ.
Calyptriform projections are noted in the specimen.

senses.definition: having a form that resembles calyptra-like coverings; varying in shape related to calyptragenous features.
examples: Calyptrimorphous features were observed in the fossil.
The term is used mainly in descriptive anatomy or paleontology.

senses.definition: a rare or archaic term-root used in compound scientific names; may appear as a combining form in taxonomy.
examples: In some texts, calyptro appears as a prefix in species names.
The term is mostly encountered in historical descriptions.

senses.definition: an organ or tissue related to calypt-like covering; used in specialized anatomical or botanical descriptions.
examples: The calyptrogen is described in the botanical text.
Further study is needed to clarify the function of the calyptrogen.

senses.definition: plural form of calyptrogen; refers to multiple calyptrogen structures in a specimen.
examples: The calibration highlighted several calyptrogens.
Researchers documented calyptrogens across the sample set.

senses.pos: noun (possibly taxonomic name or poetic usage)
senses.definition: a rarely encountered term possibly used as a proper taxonomic name or in descriptive text to denote calyptra-like forms.
examples: The specimen was labeled Calyptrogyne in the old notes.
Calypotrogyne analyses are cited in the monograph.

senses.definition: a genus of climbing plants in the evening primrose family, commonly known as bindweeds.
examples: Calystegia is native to many temperate regions.
Gardeners grow Calystegia for its trailing vines.

senses.definition: the outer whorl of a flower, consisting of sepals that protect the developing bud.
examples: The calyx protects the flower bud before it opens.
Petals emerge from the calyx as the flower blooms.

senses.definition: plural form of calyx; the outer floral envelope made of sepals.
examples: The calyxes remained closed after the flower wilted.
Notice the calyxes at the base of each flower.

senses.definition: a raised or paved road or path; in Spanish, it means road or way; used in certain geographical or architectural contexts in English texts.
examples: The old town center features a cobbled calzada.
Travelers walked along the stone calzada to reach the square.

senses.definition: a type of folded pizza filled with cheese, meats, or vegetables; popular in Italian cuisine.
examples: I ordered a cheese calzone for lunch.
Calzone is similar to a turnover but filled with pizza ingredients.

senses.definition: a plural noun form used in certain contexts to refer to people or things associated with calzone or a similar item, or a surname; may appear in culinary or fashion contexts.
examples: Calzoneras appeared on the menu as a regional dish.
In historical texts, calzoneras referred to a class of cooks.
